title: Bitter Dense Algerian Hind Shank Stir-fry
description: A one-pot dish featuring tender hind shank cooked with aromatic spices and vegetables for a rich and flavorful meal.

ingredients.name: 2 Carrots
ingredients.amount: 1 carrot, peeled and cut into 4 pieces

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup Fresh Parsley
ingredients.amount: half a cup fresh parsley leaves (chopped)

ingredients.name: 1 tbsp Ground Cumin
ingredients.amount: one tablespoon cumin seeds ground to a fine powder

instructions: Preheat a large pan or wok over medium heat and add olive oil.
Add the hind shank to the pan, season with salt (optional) and cook until browned on all sides. This should take about 10 minutes.
Remove the hind shank from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add a bit more oil if needed and sauté the crushed garlic for a minute or two, being careful not to burn.
Add sliced onions, chopped carrots, celery, and ground cumin. Cook until they start to soften slightly, about 5 minutes.
Stir in turmeric powder, followed by tomato paste, and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Return the hind shank to the pan along with any accumulated juices from its previous cooking.
Pour in the warmed vegetable stock. Bring it to a boil then reduce heat to low-medium and let simmer gently for 40 minutes covered.
Taste test the sauce, adjust seasoning if necessary (pepper is usually added). Stir in chopped parsley.
Serve hot as a main course with steamed rice or couscous.
